Class.Documentation:
AvailabilityFacade manages resource availability independently of inventory entries.

Resources can be created, locked, and unlocked without going through InventoryEntry.
Class.Documentation_plain: AvailabilityFacade manages resource availability independently of inventory entries. Resources can be created, locked, and unlocked without going through InventoryEntry.
Class.Name: AvailabilityFacade

Operation.Documentation:
Releases all expired blockades from resources that have them.

Returns list of all released blockade IDs.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Releases all expired blockades from resources that have them. Returns list of all released blockade IDs.
Operation.Name: releaseExpired

Class.Documentation:
CompositeResourceAvailability manages availability of a bundle of resources.

All components must be available for the composite to be available.

Examples: car rental (car + GPS + child seat), hotel package (room + breakfast + parking).

Competition model: all-or-nothing - all components must be locked together.
Class.Documentation_plain: CompositeResourceAvailability manages availability of a bundle of resources. All components must be available for the composite to be available. Examples: car rental (car + GPS + child seat), hotel package (room + breakfast + parking). Competition model: all-or-nothing - all components must be locked together.
Class.Name: CompositeResourceAvailability

Class.Documentation:
IndividualResourceAvailability manages availability of a single, indivisible resource.

Examples: laptop, projector, bike, parking spot.

Competition model: winner takes all - first one to lock wins, others fail.
Class.Documentation_plain: IndividualResourceAvailability manages availability of a single, indivisible resource. Examples: laptop, projector, bike, parking spot. Competition model: winner takes all - first one to lock wins, others fail.
Class.Name: IndividualResourceAvailability

Class.Documentation:
PoolResourceAvailability manages availability of a quantity-based resource pool.

Examples: milk (liters), API tokens, parking spots, consulting hours.

Competition model: multiple actors can lock portions of the pool simultaneously.
Class.Documentation_plain: PoolResourceAvailability manages availability of a quantity-based resource pool. Examples: milk (liters), API tokens, parking spots, consulting hours. Competition model: multiple actors can lock portions of the pool simultaneously.
Class.Name: PoolResourceAvailability

Class.Documentation:
ResourceAvailability is the core interface for managing resource availability.

Different implementations handle different types of resources:

- IndividualResourceAvailability: single resources (one winner takes all)

- PoolResourceAvailability: quantity-based resources (shared pool)

- TemporalResourceAvailability: time-slot based resources
Class.Documentation_plain: ResourceAvailability is the core interface for managing resource availability. Different implementations handle different types of resources: - IndividualResourceAvailability: single resources (one winner takes all) - PoolResourceAvailability: quantity-based resources (shared pool) - TemporalResourceAvailability: time-slot based resources
Class.Name: ResourceAvailability

Operation.Documentation: Checks if this resource has any expired blockades.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if this resource has any expired blockades.
Operation.Name: hasExpiredBlockades

Operation.Documentation:
Releases all expired blockades from this resource.

Returns list of released blockade IDs.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Releases all expired blockades from this resource. Returns list of released blockade IDs.
Operation.Name: releaseExpired

Class.Documentation:
TemporalResourceAvailability manages availability of a resource within a specific time slot.

Examples: hotel room for a specific night, conference room for a specific hour, appointment slot.

Competition model: winner takes all within the time slot.

Following the timed-availability pattern: one slot = one blockade.

For multi-slot reservations, use TemporalResourceGroupedAvailability.
Class.Documentation_plain:
TemporalResourceAvailability manages availability of a resource within a specific time slot. Examples: hotel room for a specific night, conference room for a specific hour, appointment slot. Competition model: winner takes all within the time slot.
Following the timed-availability pattern: one slot = one blockade. For multi-slot reservations, use TemporalResourceGroupedAvailability.
Class.Name: TemporalResourceAvailability

Class.Documentation:
TemporalResourceGroupedAvailability coordinates availability across multiple time slots.

Used for multi-slot reservations (e.g., hotel stay over multiple nights).

All slots must be available for the reservation to succeed.
Class.Documentation_plain: TemporalResourceGroupedAvailability coordinates availability across multiple time slots. Used for multi-slot reservations (e.g., hotel stay over multiple nights). All slots must be available for the reservation to succeed.
Class.Name: TemporalResourceGroupedAvailability

Class.Documentation:
Test fixture for creating availability resources.

Provides access to package-private classes for tests in other packages.
Class.Documentation_plain: Test fixture for creating availability resources. Provides access to package-private classes for tests in other packages.
Class.Name: AvailabilityFixture

Class.Documentation:
Availability facade scenarios for pool resources.

Domain: Fuel station managing fuel tanks.

Each fuel type has a tank with capacity in liters. Multiple customers

can reserve fuel simultaneously. Competition model is "shared pool".
Class.Documentation_plain:
Availability facade scenarios for pool resources. Domain: Fuel station managing fuel tanks.
Each fuel type has a tank with capacity in liters. Multiple customers can reserve fuel simultaneously. Competition model is "shared pool".
Class.Name: FuelStationScenarios

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Fleet company can reserve fuel from available tank"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Multiple fleet companies can reserve from same tank"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Reservation fails when requesting more than available"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Reservation fails when tank is exhausted by previous reservations"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Tank shows as unavailable when fully reserved"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Multiple small reservations can fill the tank"

Class.Documentation:
Availability facade scenarios for temporal resources.

Domain: Medical clinic with appointment slots.

Each doctor has defined time slots (e.g., 30-minute appointments).

Patients can book available slots, competition model is "winner takes all".
Class.Documentation_plain:
Availability facade scenarios for temporal resources. Domain: Medical clinic with appointment slots.
Each doctor has defined time slots (e.g., 30-minute appointments). Patients can book available slots, competition model is "winner takes all".
Class.Name: MedicalAppointmentScenarios

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Patient can book an available appointment slot"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Second patient cannot book already taken slot"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Patient can book different slot with same doctor"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Different doctors can have appointments at the same time"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Cancelled slot becomes available for other patients"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Patient can extend their booking (re-lock same slot)"

Class.Documentation:
Availability facade scenarios for individual resources.

Domain: Medical equipment that can be reserved by departments.

Each piece of equipment is unique and indivisible.

Competition model is "winner takes all" - first to lock wins.
Class.Documentation_plain:
Availability facade scenarios for individual resources. Domain: Medical equipment that can be reserved by departments.
Each piece of equipment is unique and indivisible. Competition model is "winner takes all" - first to lock wins.
Class.Name: MedicalEquipmentScenarios

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Second department cannot reserve already taken equipment"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Department cannot release equipment reserved by another department"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Released equipment can be reserved by another department"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Can check if specific equipment is available"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Can find available equipment by resource ID"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "First department to lock wins in race condition"

Class.Documentation: In-memory implementation of InstanceRepository for testing.
Class.Documentation_plain: In-memory implementation of InstanceRepository for testing.
Class.Name: InMemoryInstanceRepository

Class.Documentation:
Instance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a Product.

While Product defines WHAT can be sold, Instance represents WHAT WAS actually sold/created.

Common interface for:

- ProductInstance: specific instance of a ProductType (e.g., iPhone with serial ABC123)

- PackageInstance: specific instance of a PackageType (e.g., laptop bundle with customer's choices)

Every instance must be tracked by at least one of:

- SerialNumber (individual tracking)

- Batch (group tracking)
Class.Documentation_plain:
Instance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a Product. While Product defines WHAT can be sold, Instance represents WHAT WAS actually sold/created.
Common interface for: - ProductInstance: specific instance of a ProductType (e.g., iPhone with serial ABC123) - PackageInstance: specific instance of a PackageType (e.g., laptop bundle with customer's choices)
Every instance must be tracked by at least one of: - SerialNumber (individual tracking) - Batch (group tracking)
Class.Name: Instance

Operation.Documentation:
Returns the effective quantity - either the explicit quantity or 1 piece as default.

Used for counting/summing instances.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the effective quantity - either the explicit quantity or 1 piece as default. Used for counting/summing instances.
Operation.Name: effectiveQuantity

Class.Documentation:
Builder for creating ProductInstance with fluent API.

Usage:

<pre>

ProductInstance instance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.random(), productId)

.withSerial(SerialNumber.of("ABC123"))

.withQuantity(Quantity.of(1, Unit.pieces()))

.withFeature("color", "silver")

.withFeature("size", "L")

.build();

ProductInstance batchInstance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.random(), productId)

.withBatch(BatchId.random())

.build();

</pre>
Class.Documentation_plain:
Builder for creating ProductInstance with fluent API. Usage: 
ProductInstance instance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.random(), productId)
.withSerial(SerialNumber.of("ABC123"))
.withQuantity(Quantity.of(1, Unit.pieces()))
.withFeature("color", "silver")
.withFeature("size", "L")
.build();

ProductInstance batchInstance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.random(), productId)
.withBatch(BatchId.random())
.build();
Class.Name: InstanceBuilder

Class.Documentation:
InstanceCriteria is a specification pattern for filtering product instances.

Used for queries like "find all instances with color=Black" or "instances from batch X".

Supports composition via and(), or(), and not() operations.
Class.Documentation_plain: InstanceCriteria is a specification pattern for filtering product instances. Used for queries like "find all instances with color=Black" or "instances from batch X". Supports composition via and(), or(), and not() operations.
Class.Name: InstanceCriteria

Class.Documentation: Repository for ProductInstance entities.
Class.Documentation_plain: Repository for ProductInstance entities.
Class.Name: InstanceRepository

Class.Documentation:
InventoryEntry is the aggregate root that maps a product to its instances and resources.

- instances: Set of all InstanceIds belonging to this entry

- instanceToResource: Maps instances to resources for availability tracking

Not all instances need to have a resource mapping (e.g., instances not yet made available).

Actual availability is managed by AvailabilityFacade - this is just the mapping.
Class.Documentation_plain:
InventoryEntry is the aggregate root that maps a product to its instances and resources.
- instances: Set of all InstanceIds belonging to this entry - instanceToResource: Maps instances to resources for availability tracking
Not all instances need to have a resource mapping (e.g., instances not yet made available). Actual availability is managed by AvailabilityFacade - this is just the mapping.
Class.Name: InventoryEntry

Operation.Documentation: === Instance management ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Instance management ===
Operation.Name: addInstance

Operation.Documentation: === Resource mapping ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Resource mapping ===
Operation.Name: mapInstanceToResource

Operation.Documentation:
Handles lock command by delegating to appropriate handler based on resource specification.

Returns list of blockade IDs on success.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Handles lock command by delegating to appropriate handler based on resource specification. Returns list of blockade IDs on success.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation:
Handles temporal lock (e.g., hotel room for multiple nights).

Note: Current implementation takes first available resource.

Alternative strategies: find resource available for all slots,

use customer preference, load balancing, etc.
Operation.Documentation_plain:
Handles temporal lock (e.g., hotel room for multiple nights).
Note: Current implementation takes first available resource. Alternative strategies: find resource available for all slots, use customer preference, load balancing, etc.
Operation.Name: handleTemporalLock

Operation.Documentation: Handles individual lock for specific instance.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Handles individual lock for specific instance.
Operation.Name: handleIndividualLock

Operation.Documentation:
Handles quantity lock for pool resources (e.g., milk, fuel).

Note: Current implementation takes first pool resource.

Alternative strategies: distribute across multiple pools, FIFO, etc.
Operation.Documentation_plain:
Handles quantity lock for pool resources (e.g., milk, fuel).
Note: Current implementation takes first pool resource. Alternative strategies: distribute across multiple pools, FIFO, etc.
Operation.Name: handleQuantityLock

Class.Documentation:
InventoryFacade manages inventory entries and product instances.

It delegates availability operations to AvailabilityFacade.
Class.Documentation_plain: InventoryFacade manages inventory entries and product instances. It delegates availability operations to AvailabilityFacade.
Class.Name: InventoryFacade

Operation.Documentation:
Counts the total quantity of all instances for a product.

Sums effectiveQuantity() of all instances in the entry.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Counts the total quantity of all instances for a product. Sums effectiveQuantity() of all instances in the entry.
Operation.Name: countProduct

Operation.Documentation: Counts the quantity of instances matching the given criteria.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Counts the quantity of instances matching the given criteria.
Operation.Name: countProduct

Operation.Documentation: Finds instance IDs matching the given criteria for a product.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds instance IDs matching the given criteria for a product.
Operation.Name: findInstances

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a new product instance and adds it to the InventoryEntry.

Validates the instance data against the product definition via ProductDefinitionValidator.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a new product instance and adds it to the InventoryEntry. Validates the instance data against the product definition via ProductDefinitionValidator.
Operation.Name: createInstance

Operation.Documentation: === Lock handling ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Lock handling ===
Operation.Name: handle

Class.Documentation:
Validates instance data against product definition.

This interface is intended to be implemented by calling the Product archetype

to verify that:

- The product identifier exists

- The tracking strategy matches the product definition

- The features are valid for this product type

- Required features are provided

- Feature values match the allowed values/ranges defined in the product type

Example implementation:

<pre>

class ProductArchetypeValidator implements ProductDefinitionValidator {

private final ProductFacade productFacade;

@Override

public Result<String, Void> validate(ProductIdentifier productId,

ProductTrackingStrategy strategy,

Map<String, String> features) {

// Call product archetype to validate

return productFacade.validateInstanceData(productId, strategy, features);

}

}

</pre>
Class.Name: ProductDefinitionValidator

Operation.Documentation: Validates instance data against the product definition.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates instance data against the product definition.
Operation.Name: validate
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: success if valid, failure with error message if invalid
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: success if valid, failure with error message if invalid

Parameter.Documentation: the feature values for this instance
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the feature values for this instance
Parameter.Name: features

Operation.Documentation:
A permissive validator that accepts all data.

Useful for testing or when product validation is not required.
Operation.Documentation_plain: A permissive validator that accepts all data. Useful for testing or when product validation is not required.
Operation.Name: alwaysValid

Class.Documentation:
ProductInstance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a ProductType in inventory.

Examples:

- ProductType: "iPhone 15 Pro 256GB" -> ProductInstance: specific phone with serial ABC123

- ProductType: "Organic Milk 1L" -> ProductInstance: specific bottle from batch LOT-2024-001

- ProductType: "Consulting" -> ProductInstance: 8.5 hours of consulting delivered

Each instance must be tracked by at least one of:

- SerialNumber (individual tracking)

- Batch (group tracking)

- Or both

Optional quantity tracks the amount for this specific instance (e.g., 8.5 hours, 3.2 kg).

Features map contains actual values for features defined in the ProductType

(e.g., color=red, size=L, yearOfProduction=2023).
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductInstance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a ProductType in inventory.
Examples: - ProductType: "iPhone 15 Pro 256GB" -> ProductInstance: specific phone with serial ABC123 - ProductType: "Organic Milk 1L" -> ProductInstance: specific bottle from batch LOT-2024-001 - ProductType: "Consulting" -> ProductInstance: 8.5 hours of consulting delivered
Each instance must be tracked by at least one of: - SerialNumber (individual tracking) - Batch (group tracking) - Or both
Optional quantity tracks the amount for this specific instance (e.g., 8.5 hours, 3.2 kg).
Features map contains actual values for features defined in the ProductType (e.g., color=red, size=L, yearOfProduction=2023).
Class.Name: ProductInstance

Class.Documentation:
Local representation of product tracking strategy in Inventory module.

Defines how product instances are tracked and identified.
Class.Documentation_plain: Local representation of product tracking strategy in Inventory module. Defines how product instances are tracked and identified.
Class.Name: ProductTrackingStrategy

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
One-of-a-kind item (e.g., Mona Lisa painting).

Always exactly one instance exists.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: One-of-a-kind item (e.g., Mona Lisa painting). Always exactly one instance exists.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: UNIQUE

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: Each instance has unique serial number (e.g., phones, laptops).
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Each instance has unique serial number (e.g., phones, laptops).
EnumerationLiteral.Name: INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: Instances are tracked by production batch (e.g., milk, medicines).
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Instances are tracked by production batch (e.g., milk, medicines).
EnumerationLiteral.Name: BATCH_TRACKED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: Both individual serial and batch tracking (e.g., high-value items in batches).
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Both individual serial and batch tracking (e.g., high-value items in batches).
EnumerationLiteral.Name: INDIVIDUALLY_AND_BATCH_TRACKED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
Fully interchangeable instances (e.g., electricity, water, sand).

No serial numbers or batches - pure quantity.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Fully interchangeable instances (e.g., electricity, water, sand). No serial numbers or batches - pure quantity.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: IDENTICAL

Class.Documentation:
Reservation is the published language layer on top of resource availability.

It represents a customer's claim on one or more resources for a specific purpose.

A single reservation can block multiple resources (e.g., hotel room for 4 nights).
Class.Documentation_plain:
Reservation is the published language layer on top of resource availability. It represents a customer's claim on one or more resources for a specific purpose.
A single reservation can block multiple resources (e.g., hotel room for 4 nights).
Class.Name: Reservation

Class.Documentation:
ReservationFacade is the published language layer for reservations.

It translates between business reservation concepts and inventory locks.
Class.Documentation_plain: ReservationFacade is the published language layer for reservations. It translates between business reservation concepts and inventory locks.
Class.Name: ReservationFacade

Class.Documentation: Purpose of the reservation - used for categorization and querying.
Class.Documentation_plain: Purpose of the reservation - used for categorization and querying.
Class.Name: ReservationPurpose

Class.Documentation:
Reservation scenarios for pool resources.

Domain: Fleet fuel reservations at a fuel station.
Class.Documentation_plain: Reservation scenarios for pool resources. Domain: Fleet fuel reservations at a fuel station.
Class.Name: FuelReservationScenarios

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Fleet can reserve fuel from available tank"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Multiple fleets can reserve from same tank"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Reservation fails when requesting more than available"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Reservation fails when tank exhausted by previous reservations"

Class.Documentation:
Reservation scenarios for temporal resources.

Domain: Hotel room reservations with nightly slots.
Class.Documentation_plain: Reservation scenarios for temporal resources. Domain: Hotel room reservations with nightly slots.
Class.Name: HotelReservationScenarios

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Guest can reserve room for one night"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Second guest cannot reserve same room for same night"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Guest can reserve room for multiple consecutive nights"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Reservation fails if any night in range is taken"

JavaAnnotationProperty.Name: value
JavaAnnotationProperty.Value: "Guest can cancel and room becomes available"

Class.Documentation: SerialNumber uniquely identifies an individual instance.
Class.Documentation_plain: SerialNumber uniquely identifies an individual instance.
Class.Name: SerialNumber

Class.Documentation:
Criteria-based selection.

Finds first entry that matches the predicate from context.

Order is preserved (FIFO among matching entries).
Class.Documentation_plain: Criteria-based selection. Finds first entry that matches the predicate from context. Order is preserved (FIFO among matching entries).
Class.Name: CriteriaSelectionPolicy

Class.Documentation:
FIFO - First In, First Out.

Simply returns the head of the queue.
Class.Documentation_plain: FIFO - First In, First Out. Simply returns the head of the queue.
Class.Name: FifoSelectionPolicy

Class.Documentation:
Priority-based selection.

Relies on PriorityQueue ordering (entries must be Comparable).

Higher priority (lower number) comes first, FIFO within same priority.
Class.Documentation_plain: Priority-based selection. Relies on PriorityQueue ordering (entries must be Comparable). Higher priority (lower number) comes first, FIFO within same priority.
Class.Name: PrioritySelectionPolicy

Class.Documentation:
Quota-based selection policy.

Ensures fair distribution across segments (e.g., VIP vs Standard).

Each segment has a quota - max number of entries that can be fulfilled per period.
Class.Documentation_plain: Quota-based selection policy. Ensures fair distribution across segments (e.g., VIP vs Standard). Each segment has a quota - max number of entries that can be fulfilled per period.
Class.Name: QuotaSelectionPolicy

Class.Documentation:
Generic waitlist with configurable selection policy.

Supports FIFO, Priority, and Criteria-based selection.
Class.Documentation_plain: Generic waitlist with configurable selection policy. Supports FIFO, Priority, and Criteria-based selection.
Class.Name: WaitList

Class.Documentation:
Strategy for selecting next entry from waitlist.

@param <T> type of payload in waitlist entries
Class.Documentation_plain:
Strategy for selecting next entry from waitlist.
@param <T> type of payload in waitlist entries
Class.Name: WaitListSelectionPolicy

Operation.Documentation: Select next entry from queue based on policy logic.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Select next entry from queue based on policy logic.
Operation.Name: selectNext
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: selected entry wrapped in Optional, empty if none can be selected
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: selected entry wrapped in Optional, empty if none can be selected

Parameter.Documentation: mutable queue (policy may modify it - remove element)
Parameter.Documentation_plain: mutable queue (policy may modify it - remove element)
Parameter.Name: queue

Operation.Documentation: === InventoryEntry tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === InventoryEntry tests ===
Operation.Name: createsInventoryEntry

Operation.Documentation: === Instance creation tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Instance creation tests ===
Operation.Name: createsInstanceAndAddsToEntry

Operation.Documentation: === Instance to Resource mapping tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Instance to Resource mapping tests ===
Operation.Name: mapsInstanceToResource

Operation.Documentation: === Remove instance tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Remove instance tests ===
Operation.Name: removesInstanceFromEntry

Operation.Documentation: === Instance query tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Instance query tests ===
Operation.Name: findsInstanceBySerialNumber

Operation.Documentation: === Counting tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Counting tests ===
Operation.Name: countsIndividuallyTrackedProducts

Operation.Documentation: === Find resources for product tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Find resources for product tests ===
Operation.Name: findsResourcesForProduct

Operation.Documentation: === Duplicate entry prevention ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Duplicate entry prevention ===
Operation.Name: failsToCreateDuplicateEntryForSameProduct

Operation.Documentation: === Error handling ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === Error handling ===
Operation.Name: failsToMapResourceForNonexistentEntry

Operation.Documentation: === LockCommand handling tests ===
Operation.Documentation_plain: === LockCommand handling tests ===
Operation.Name: handlesIndividualLockCommand
